message.content:
Decompose this research question into complementary search angles.

## Question
Identify and rank the most impactful benchmarks, leaderboards, and "arenas" for evaluating AI DEEP-RESEARCH agents — systems that autonomously search the web and academic sources, then write long-form, fully-cited research reports (competitors: OpenAI Deep Research, Google Gemini Deep Research, Perplexity Deep Research, xAI, Anthropic). As of 2026.

CONTEXT / WHY: We operate our own deep-research agent and currently measure ourselves only with DeepResearch-Bench (its RACE report-quality score and FACT citation-support score). We want the FULL competitive scoreboard landscape so we can (a) prioritize which benchmarks to optimize for, and (b) know where an external team can actually deploy/submit a score for public credibility and visibility.

FOR EACH benchmark / leaderboard / arena, report:
1. Name, the organization or people who run it, and its canonical URL.
2. What it measures — task type (open-web browsing QA, long-form cited report quality, citation faithfulness/hallucination, tool-use, multi-hop reasoning) and the exact metric(s) it reports.
3. How scores are produced and whether an OUTSIDE team can be scored: is there a public leaderboard, an automatic LLM judge, a formal submission/PR process, a held-out test set, or head-to-head human/arena voting? Is the harness open-source and self-runnable?
4. Impact / visibility — who currently appears on it (which labs/products), whether major labs cite it in launches or papers, and roughly how much industry attention it has.
5. Known strengths, limitations, and how gameable the metric is.

SPECIFICALLY INVESTIGATE AND DISAMBIGUATE:
- "arena.ai" — what is it exactly? Distinguish clearly from LMArena / lmarena.ai (formerly Chatbot Arena / LMSYS) and from any separate "Deep Research Arena". Report what actually lives at arena.ai.
- LMArena / Chatbot Arena: does it have a deep-research, web-search, or "search arena" category where a full research agent (not just a chat model) can compete? How does submission work?
- DeepResearch Bench (RACE + FACT) — who maintains it, is there a public leaderboard, how do you submit.
- GAIA (general assistant benchmark), BrowseComp and BrowseComp-Plus (OpenAI), Humanity's Last Exam (HLE), FRAMES, SimpleQA, WebArena / WebVoyager / Mind2Web, ResearchQA, DeepConsult, and any academic "Deep Research Arena" or agent-report evaluation.
- Any venue specifically designed to score or vote on autonomous deep-research REPORTS head-to-head (the closest analog to an "arena" for our product).

DELIVERABLE: a RANKED shortlist (most impactful first) of the venues/benchmarks that BOTH (a) carry the most credibility/visibility in the field, AND (b) actually allow an external deep-research system to be scored or submitted. For each, give the risk/effort of getting on it. End with a clear TOP RECOMMENDATION of the 2-3 venues we should target first and exactly why, plus a note on which are pure internal-measurement tools versus public leaderboards we can climb.

## Task
Generate 5 distinct web search queries that together cover the question from different angles. Pick angles that suit the question's domain. Examples:
- broad/primary  · academic/technical  · recent news  · contrarian/skeptical  · practitioner/implementation
- For medical: anatomy · common causes · serious differentials · authoritative refs · red flags
- For tech: state-of-art · benchmarks · limitations · industry adoption · cost/tradeoffs

Make queries specific enough to surface high-signal results. Avoid redundancy.
Return: the question (verbatim or lightly normalized), a 1-2 sentence decomposition strategy, and the angles.

Structured output only.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: This is a research-decomposition task. I don't need to explore the codebase — I need to produce 5 complementary search angles for the deep-research question about evaluation venues for AI deep-research agents.

input.question:
Identify and rank the most impactful benchmarks, leaderboards, and "arenas" (as of 2026) for evaluating autonomous AI deep-research agents — systems that search the web and academic sources then write long-form, fully-cited reports (OpenAI Deep Research, Gemini Deep Research, Perplexity, xAI, Anthropic). For each venue: what it measures and its metrics; whether an outside team can be scored/submitted (public leaderboard, LLM judge, PR/submission process, held-out set, human/arena voting, open-source harness); its industry impact/visibility; and its strengths/limitations/gameability. Specifically disambiguate arena.ai vs LMArena/Chatbot Arena vs any "Deep Research Arena," and cover DeepResearch-Bench (RACE+FACT), GAIA, BrowseComp/BrowseComp-Plus, HLE, FRAMES, SimpleQA, WebArena/WebVoyager/Mind2Web, ResearchQA, DeepConsult. Deliver a ranked shortlist of venues that are both high-credibility AND externally submittable, with risk/effort per venue and a top 2-3 recommendation, noting which are internal-measurement-only vs public leaderboards.</question>
<parameter name="summary">Decomposition strategy: The question spans two axes — (1) an enumeration of many specific named benchmarks/leaderboards that must each be characterized, and (2) a meta-question about which venues are actually externally submittable and carry field credibility. I split the angles to (a) directly disambiguate the "arena" entities (arena.ai vs LMArena vs Deep Research Arena) since that's an explicit high-priority ask, (b) cover the report-quality/citation-faithfulness benchmarks closest to the user's product (DeepResearch-Bench, DeepConsult, ResearchQA), (c) cover the web-browsing/agentic QA benchmarks the major labs actually cite in launches (GAIA, BrowseComp, HLE, FRAMES, SimpleQA), (d) surface the submission mechanics and open-source harness/leaderboard status, and (e) pull recent lab-launch and industry-signal evidence to rank impact and detect gameability critiques.</summary>
<parameter name="angles">[{"label":"Arena disambiguation (primary)","query":"arena.ai vs LMArena lmarena.ai Chatbot Arena LMSYS search arena deep research arena difference 2026","rationale":"Directly resolves the explicit disambiguation ask: what actually lives at arena.ai, how it differs from LMArena/Chatbot Arena, and whether a distinct 'Deep Research Arena' exists. High-priority named-entity confusion the user flagged."},{"label":"Report-quality & citation benchmarks (closest to product)","query":"DeepResearch Bench RACE FACT leaderboard submission DeepConsult ResearchQA long-form cited report evaluation LLM judge","rationale":"Covers the benchmarks that measure long-form cited report quality and citation faithfulness — the exact category the user's product competes in — plus how to submit and who maintains the leaderboards."},{"label":"Web-browsing & agentic QA benchmarks labs cite","query":"BrowseComp BrowseComp-Plus GAIA Humanity's Last Exam FRAMES SimpleQA benchmark OpenAI Gemini deep research agent evaluation","rationale":"Enumerates the open-web browsing and multi-hop QA benchmarks that major labs actually cite in Deep Research product launches, establishing which carry the most industry credibility."},{"label":"Submission mechanics & open-source harnesses","query":"WebArena WebVoyager Mind2Web open source harness held-out test set public leaderboard submit external agent score reproducible","rationale":"Addresses criterion (3): which harnesses are open-source and self-runnable, which have held-out sets or formal submission/PR processes, so we know where an external team can actually deploy and post a score."},{"label":"Industry impact, ranking, and gameability critiques","query":"deep research agent benchmark leaderboard 2025 2026 contamination gaming criticism which benchmarks labs cite launch OpenAI Perplexity xAI Anthropic","rationale":"Provides the recency and skeptical signal needed to rank venues by real visibility and to report each metric's limitations and gameability — including contamination and LLM-judge bias critiques."}]

content.content: Output does not match required schema: root: must have required property 'angles', root: must have required property 'summary'

content.text: The schema wants `summary`, `question`, and `angles`, but the validator flagged them missing — likely because the `rationale` fields nested inside angles conflicted or the top-level keys weren't recognized. Let me retry with a cleaner shape.

input.question:
Identify and rank the most impactful benchmarks, leaderboards, and arenas (as of 2026) for evaluating autonomous AI deep-research agents that search the web and academic sources then write long-form fully-cited reports (OpenAI Deep Research, Gemini Deep Research, Perplexity, xAI, Anthropic). For each venue: what it measures and its metrics; whether an outside team can be scored/submitted (public leaderboard, LLM judge, submission process, held-out set, human/arena voting, open-source harness); its industry impact/visibility; and its strengths/limitations/gameability. Disambiguate arena.ai vs LMArena/Chatbot Arena vs any Deep Research Arena, and cover DeepResearch-Bench (RACE+FACT), GAIA, BrowseComp/BrowseComp-Plus, HLE, FRAMES, SimpleQA, WebArena/WebVoyager/Mind2Web, ResearchQA, DeepConsult. Deliver a ranked shortlist of venues that are both high-credibility AND externally submittable, with risk/effort per venue and a top 2-3 recommendation, noting internal-measurement-only vs public leaderboards.</question>
<parameter name="summary">Two axes drive the decomposition: enumerating and characterizing many specific named benchmarks, and answering the meta-question of which venues are externally submittable and field-credible. Angles split across the explicit arena disambiguation, the report-quality/citation benchmarks closest to the product, the browsing/agentic-QA benchmarks labs cite in launches, submission mechanics and open-source harness status, and recent lab-launch signals plus gameability critiques for ranking.</parameter>
<parameter name="angles">[{"label":"Arena disambiguation","query":"arena.ai vs LMArena lmarena.ai Chatbot Arena LMSYS search arena deep research arena difference 2026","rationale":"Resolves the explicit named-entity confusion: what lives at arena.ai, how it differs from LMArena/Chatbot Arena, and whether a distinct Deep Research Arena exists."},{"label":"Report-quality and citation benchmarks","query":"DeepResearch Bench RACE FACT leaderboard submission DeepConsult ResearchQA long-form cited report evaluation LLM judge","rationale":"Covers benchmarks measuring long-form cited report quality and citation faithfulness — the product's exact category — plus submission and maintainer details."},{"label":"Browsing and agentic QA benchmarks labs cite","query":"BrowseComp BrowseComp-Plus GAIA Humanity's Last Exam FRAMES SimpleQA benchmark OpenAI Gemini deep research agent evaluation","rationale":"Enumerates open-web browsing and multi-hop QA benchmarks major labs cite in Deep Research launches, establishing industry credibility."},{"label":"Submission mechanics and open-source harnesses","query":"WebArena WebVoyager Mind2Web open source harness held-out test set public leaderboard submit external agent score reproducible","rationale":"Identifies which harnesses are open-source/self-runnable and which offer held-out sets or formal submission, showing where an external team can actually post a score."},{"label":"Industry impact and gameability critiques","query":"deep research agent benchmark leaderboard 2025 2026 contamination gaming criticism which benchmarks labs cite launch OpenAI Perplexity xAI Anthropic","rationale":"Supplies recency and skeptical signal to rank venues by real visibility and report each metric's limitations, contamination, and LLM-judge bias."}]
